name: Baby Ruth Layer Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt candy bars in the top of a double boiler.
2. Cream butter and sugar thoroughly until light and fluffy.
3. Add well beaten eggs and beat until combined.
4. Blend in the cooled candy bar mix and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla.
5. Combine flour, baking soda, and baking powder; mix well.
6. Alternately add dry ingredients with buttermilk, blending on low speed after each addition until just combined.
7. Pour into 2 buttered and floured 9-inch round layer pans.
8. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for about 30 minutes.
9. Cool in pans on a rack for 10 minutes.
10. Turn out onto a rack to finish cooling.
Baby Ruth Filling & Topping:
1. In a saucepan, combine egg yolks, evaporated milk, sugar, and butter.
2. Cook over medium heat until thickened, about 10 minutes.
3. Remove from heat and blend in melted candy bars, coconut, and peanuts.
4. Cool completely.
Whipped Topping:
1. Whip heavy cream stiffly.
2. Fold in stiffly beaten egg white, powdered sugar, and vanilla.
Assembly:
1. Spread cooled filling between layers and on top of the cake.
2. Spread whipped topping on the sides of the cake.
3. Cut carefully with a sharp knife.
